structuralBlockers: Requires relocation to Austin or Fremont; not addressable by self-study
Demands demonstrated production experience in real-time or low-latency systems; self-taught prototypes insufficient

alternativeTargetBranches.rationale: Fits current full-stack + Python/ML prototyping skills while building domain exposure without Neuralink's hardware integration or relocation demands.
alternativeTargetBranches.tradeoffs: Lower compensation ceiling and less direct impact on cutting-edge implants; slower skill growth in low-latency neural decoding.

alternativeTargetBranches.rationale: Leverages agentic AI and LLM experience for building internal research tooling at neuro or biotech labs.
alternativeTargetBranches.tradeoffs: Further from user-facing BCI applications; reduced ownership of end-to-end participant sessions.

clusters.name: Neuroscience & BCI Domain Fundamentals
clusters.description: Core concepts from neuroscience and brain-computer interface literature required to interpret papers and collaborate with domain experts.

subSkills.name: Basic Neurophysiology
subSkills.description: Neurons, action potentials, and cortical organization relevant to motor and sensory BCIs.

concepts.name: Action Potential Generation
concepts.description: Explain ion channel dynamics and spike initiation; map to extracellular recording signals.

suggestedResources.title: Principles of Neural Science, 5th ed. - Kandel et al., Chapters 6-9

concepts.name: Cortical Motor Areas
concepts.description: Identify primary motor cortex, premotor areas, and their role in movement intent decoding.

suggestedResources.title: Principles of Neural Science, 5th ed. - Kandel et al., Chapters 37-38
Georgopoulos et al. 1986 - Neuronal population coding of movement direction

concepts.name: Spike Sorting Basics
concepts.description: Distinguish single-unit vs multi-unit activity and basic clustering approaches used in BCI recordings.

suggestedResources.title: Lewicki 1998 - A review of methods for spike sorting

concepts.name: BCI Signal Types
concepts.description: Compare ECoG, intracortical spikes, and EEG for bandwidth, invasiveness, and decoding potential.

suggestedResources.title: Wolpaw et al. 2002 - Brain-computer interfaces for communication and control

subSkills.name: Motor Imagery & Intent Decoding
subSkills.description: Link participant mental strategies to measurable neural correlates used in real-time control.

concepts.name: Motor Imagery Paradigms
concepts.description: Distinguish imagined vs attempted movement and resulting mu/beta rhythm changes.

suggestedResources.title: Pfurtscheller & Neuper 2001 - Motor imagery and direct brain-computer communication

concepts.name: Population Vector Decoding
concepts.description: Implement basic direction decoding from tuned neuron populations as in early BCI work.

suggestedResources.title: Georgopoulos et al. 1986 - Neuronal population coding

concepts.name: Calibration Sessions
concepts.description: Design data collection protocols that map neural activity to intended commands.

suggestedResources.title: Hochberg et al. 2006 - Neuronal ensemble control of prosthetic devices

concepts.name: Closed-Loop Feedback
concepts.description: Explain why immediate sensory or visual feedback is required for participant learning.

suggestedResources.title: Carmena et al. 2003 - Learning to control a brain-machine interface

suggestedArtefact.title: Annotated summary of 5 seminal BCI papers with pseudocode for each decoding approach
suggestedArtefact.description: Produce a technical memo that extracts implementable algorithms from primary literature.

clusters.name: Low-Latency Real-Time Systems
clusters.description: Engineering patterns for deterministic, sub-100ms pipelines from implant to UI.

subSkills.name: Real-Time Data Pipelines
subSkills.description: Streaming architectures that guarantee bounded latency from hardware to application layer.

concepts.name: Ring Buffers & Lock-Free Queues
concepts.description: Implement producer-consumer patterns without GC pauses or mutex contention.

suggestedResources.title: Java Concurrency in Practice - Goetz et al.

concepts.name: Deterministic Scheduling
concepts.description: Use real-time OS primitives or thread priorities to bound jitter.

concepts.name: Zero-Copy Networking
concepts.description: Move data from kernel to user space without memcpy for sensor streams.

suggestedResources.title: High Performance Browser Networking - Ilya Grigorik

concepts.name: Latency Measurement
concepts.description: Instrument end-to-end timing and set SLOs for participant-visible actions.

suggestedResources.title: Brendan Gregg - Systems Performance, 2nd ed. Chapter 5

subSkills.name: Hardware Abstraction
subSkills.description: Interface cleanly with custom implant drivers and FPGA outputs.

concepts.name: Memory-Mapped I/O
concepts.description: Read hardware registers and DMA buffers directly from user space.

suggestedResources.title: Linux Device Drivers, 3rd ed. - Corbet et al.

concepts.name: Event-Driven Architectures
concepts.description: React to spike events or feature vectors without polling loops.

suggestedResources.title: The C++ Programming Language, 4th ed. - Stroustrup (concurrency chapter)

concepts.name: Time Synchronization
concepts.description: Align implant timestamps with host clock across USB or wireless links.

concepts.name: Fail-Safe Degradation
concepts.description: Define graceful fallback modes when sensor packets are lost.

suggestedResources.title: Designing Fault-Tolerant Systems - Google SRE Chapter 22

suggestedArtefact.title: Real-time spike stream simulator with <20ms end-to-end latency to cursor control
suggestedArtefact.description: Build and benchmark a minimal pipeline using Python/C++ that mimics implant output.

clusters.name: Cross-Disciplinary Communication
clusters.description: Skills for translating between code, neuroscience papers, and clinical operators during weekly sessions.

subSkills.name: Technical Design Documentation
subSkills.description: Write engineering docs that neuroscientists and hardware engineers can review and approve.

concepts.name: Writing for Mixed Audiences
concepts.description: Structure docs so both domain experts and software engineers extract needed information.

suggestedResources.title: The Elements of Style - Strunk & White

concepts.name: Paper-to-Code Translation
concepts.description: Summarize methods sections into concrete implementation tickets.

suggestedResources.title: Distill.pub: How to read a paper

concepts.name: Live Session Debriefs
concepts.description: Capture participant feedback and turn observations into next-day code changes.

concepts.name: Pair Debugging with Domain Experts
concepts.description: Run joint debugging sessions where signal anomalies are diagnosed together.

suggestedResources.title: Julia Evans - Debugging Zine and talks
Stripe Engineering Blog - On-call and collaboration

subSkills.name: Research Implementation Workflow
subSkills.description: Process for turning primary literature directly into production-grade code.

concepts.name: Reproducibility Setup
concepts.description: Replicate published figures before extending the method.

concepts.name: Evaluation Against Baselines
concepts.description: Compare new decoder against published benchmarks using same datasets.

concepts.name: Versioned Experiment Tracking
concepts.description: Log every hyperparameter and data split so results are auditable.

concepts.name: Participant Data Ethics
concepts.description: Handle neural data under IRB and FDA constraints.

suggestedArtefact.title: Open PR to an existing BCI toolbox (e.g. MOABB or Elephant) implementing one missing decoder
suggestedArtefact.description: Demonstrates both code quality and domain understanding via public review.

clusters.name: Signal Processing for Neural Data
clusters.description: Core DSP techniques used to clean and extract features from raw implant streams.

subSkills.name: Filtering & Artifact Removal
subSkills.description: Apply causal filters that preserve timing for closed-loop use.

concepts.name: Causal vs Non-causal Filters
concepts.description: Implement real-time IIR filters without lookahead.

suggestedResources.title: Digital Signal Processing - Oppenheim & Schafer, 4th ed.

concepts.name: Feature Extraction
concepts.description: Compute bandpower, spike rates, and local field potentials usable by decoders.

suggestedResources.title: Blankertz et al. 2008 - Optimizing spatial filters

concepts.name: Dimensionality Reduction
concepts.description: Apply PCA/CCA to high-channel count data while maintaining real-time constraints.

suggestedResources.title: Pattern Recognition and Machine Learning - Bishop, Chapter 12

concepts.name: Online Adaptation
concepts.description: Update model parameters incrementally as participant performance drifts.

suggestedResources.title: Vidaurre et al. 2011 - Online adaptation in BCI

subSkills.name: UX for Thought-Controlled Interfaces
subSkills.description: Application-layer patterns that turn decoded intent into reliable, low-frustration user experiences.

concepts.name: Cursor & Click Mapping
concepts.description: Translate continuous velocity commands into stable pointer control with dwell or neural click.

suggestedResources.title: Collinger et al. 2013 - High-performance neuroprosthetic control

concepts.name: Error Correction & Undo
concepts.description: Detect and recover from misclassifications without breaking participant flow.

concepts.name: Calibration UI Flows
concepts.description: Guide participants through data collection with minimal cognitive load.

concepts.name: Accessibility & Fatigue Mitigation
concepts.description: Design sessions that account for attention limits and muscle artifacts.

suggestedResources.title: W3C Accessibility Guidelines for cognitive load

suggestedArtefact.title: Web-based BCI cursor demo using keyboard-emulated neural input with calibration wizard
suggestedArtefact.description: Full-stack prototype connecting simulated decoder output to browser events.
